$description: Validator input (design-constitution §23) — NOT tokens; deliberately outside the DTCG files. The validator (built when the compiler lands, Phase C) fails `make check` when the system contradicts its own reasoning. minLc per APCA-W3. Every check runs per theme; stage checks run across the stage's full adjustable range.

text.$comment: Every declared ink/surface pair meets its promised Lc at the smallest declared text size. Targets are per-POLARITY since the 2026-07-17 adjudication (a theme is dark ⇔ its chrome familyDirection is 'raise'): the 07-13 dark ink retune (graphite ink.2 0.80→0.75, carbon 0.785→0.74, ink.3 with them) was eye-ratified on probes and STANDS over the pre-retune single-target hypothesis; APCA scores reverse polarity lower for the same felt step, and light-on-dark bloom is why dark type runs dimmer. minLcDark applies on dark themes; absent = one target for both polarities. Dark floors sit with regression margin under the measured retuned worst case (ink.2: 51.1 on graphite surface.transient — the RAISED transient is the binding surface on dark, not panel).

separation.$comment: §3/§7/§23: EVERY adjacent step in EVERY register family sits inside the band — above the perceivability floor AND below the delta cap (spot-checking two pairs proved insufficient). ΔL is the instrument because APCA clamps sub-threshold contrast to 0. KNOWN FAILURES on 2026-07-12 hypothesis values: paper cell hover→selected (0.009) and selected→active (0.007) — re-tuned in the palette round.

structure: state deltas are authored and adjusted in multiples of the per-world register-step token (worlds/*.tokens.json); ad-hoc L nudges are drift
register families are monotonic in their declared direction (registries.json familyDirection)
every spacing/size value is a quantum multiple (strokes, icon-stroke, and type details are registered sub-grid exceptions)
the attention hue keeps >= minHueDistanceDeg from every enabled label hue (inequality alone passes hues indistinguishable at swatch scale)
chrome chroma ~ 0 outside the hue ledger rows
no theme surface sits in the dead band L 0.45-0.65 (§1)
text contracts hold across the stage adjustable range [stage.min, stage.max], not just stage.default

ring.$comment: Accent eligibility gate (2026-07-13): every hue offered in the accent picker must pass focus-ring contrast against every theme's panel. Non-text target; minLc 45 stays a PIN hypothesis. Re-based 2026-07-17 onto the world-varying ring STEP (worlds/*.tokens.json): world-independent solids measure Lc 20–43 on dark panels — unsatisfiable for ANY solid — so ring/link/toggle signals bind color.<hue>.ring while swatches keep the invariant solid.

selected-text.$comment: Text carried by the strengthened selected fill (2026-07-13, +2 register steps). Adjudicated 2026-07-17: the PROMOTE RULE (§25) — on selected fills, content ink promotes one step (ink.2 content renders ink.1, ink.3 renders ink.2), so these pairs check the PROMOTED inks: ink.1 as primary + promoted-secondary (70 not 75 — linen measures 70.4; PIN pending the linen probe in the token-gaps round), ink.2 as the promoted-muted floor.

tag.$comment: Per hue × per world (ratified 2026-07-13): chip-internal legibility for every recipe in registries.json tagRecipes.

minHueDistanceDeg.$comment: §23 PIN hypothesis. Passing as of 2026-07-13: attention = magenta (345), 40° from purple (305) and red (25).
